id: strоВЪ

Identifier for the container used in this request

expires\_at: datetimeоВЪ

The time at which the container will expire.

оА™

skills: Optional[List[[BetaSkill](api/beta.md)]]оВЪ

Skills loaded in the container

skill\_id: strоВЪ

Skill ID

оА™

type: Literal["anthropic", "custom"]оВЪ

Type of skill - either 'anthropic' (built-in) or 'custom' (user-defined)

One of the following:

"anthropic"оВЪ

"custom"оВЪ

version: strоВЪ

Skill version or 'latest' for most recent version

оА™

content: List[[BetaContentBlock](api/beta.md)]оВЪ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оБЦ

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оБЦ

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оБЦ

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaTextBlock: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]оВЪ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaCitationCharLocation: вА¶оВЪ

cited\_text: strоВЪ

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_index: intоВЪ

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]оВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

encrypted\_index: strоВЪ

url: strоВЪ

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оА™

search\_result\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

source: strоВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

title: Optional[str]оВЪ

type: Literal["search\_result\_location"]оВЪ

text: strоВЪ

type: Literal["text"]оВЪ

is\_error: boolоВЪ

tool\_use\_id: strоВЪ

type: Literal["mcp\_tool\_result"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaContainerUploadBlock: вА¶оВЪ

Response model for a file uploaded to the container.

file\_id: strоВЪ

type: Literal["container\_upload"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCompactionBlock: вА¶оВЪ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

content: Optional[str]оВЪ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

encrypted\_content: Optional[str]оВЪ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

type: Literal["compaction"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaFallbackBlock: вА¶оВЪ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn where no preceding model ran and declined has no such
boundary and carries no block вАФ the signal for whether a fallback model
served the response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оА™

from\_: [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)оВЪ

The model whose output ends at this point вАФ the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оА™

strоВЪ

оА™

role: Literal["assistant"]оВЪ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оА™

stop\_details: Optional[BetaRefusalStopDetails]оВЪ

Structured information about a refusal.

оА™

category: Optional[Literal["cyber", "bio", "frontier\_llm", "reasoning\_extraction"]]оВЪ

The policy category that triggered a refusal.

One of the following:

"cyber"оВЪ

"bio"оВЪ

"frontier\_llm"оВЪ

"reasoning\_extraction"оВЪ

оА™

explanation: Optional[str]оВЪ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оА™

fallback\_credit\_token: Optional[str]оВЪ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вАФ which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вАФ if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оА™

fallback\_has\_prefill\_claim: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вАФ
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

recommended\_model: Optional[str]оВЪ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

type: Literal["refusal"]оВЪ

оА™

stop\_reason: Optional[BetaStopReason]оВЪ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

"end\_turn"оВЪ

"max\_tokens"оВЪ

"stop\_sequence"оВЪ

"tool\_use"оВЪ

"pause\_turn"оВЪ

"compaction"оВЪ

"refusal"оВЪ

"model\_context\_window\_exceeded"оВЪ

оА™

stop\_sequence: Optional[str]оВЪ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оА™

type: Literal["message"]оВЪ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оА™

usage: [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)оВЪ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

inference\_geo: Optional[str]оВЪ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оА™

iterations: Optional[BetaIterationsUsage]оВЪ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaMessageIterationUsage: вА¶оВЪ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оА™

class BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage: вА¶оВЪ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

type: Literal["fallback\_message"]оВЪ

Usage for the fallback-model attempt that served the response

output\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of output tokens which were used.

оА™

output\_tokens\_details: Optional[BetaOutputTokensDetails]оВЪ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вАФ for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оА™

thinking\_tokens: intоВЪ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always вЙ§ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оА™

The number of server tool requests.

web\_fetch\_requests: intоВЪ

The number of web fetch tool requests.

web\_search\_requests: intоВЪ

The number of web search tool requests.

оА™

"priority"оВЪ

content: List[[BetaContentBlock](api/beta.md)]оВЪ

Content generated by the model.

This is an array of content blocks, each of which has a `type` that determines its shape.

Example:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "Hi, I'm Claude."}]
```

оБЦ

If the request input `messages` ended with an `assistant` turn, then the response `content` will continue directly from that last turn. You can use this to constrain the model's output.

For example, if the input `messages` were:

```shiki
[
  {"role": "user", "content": "What's the Greek name for Sun? (A) Sol (B) Helios (C) Sun"},
  {"role": "assistant", "content": "The best answer is ("}
]
```

оБЦ

Then the response `content` might be:

```shiki
[{"type": "text", "text": "B)"}]
```

оБЦ

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaTextBlock: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]оВЪ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaCitationCharLocation: вА¶оВЪ

cited\_text: strоВЪ

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

end\_char\_index: intоВЪ

file\_id: Optional[str]оВЪ

start\_char\_index: intоВЪ

type: Literal["char\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationPageLocation: вА¶оВЪ

cited\_text: strоВЪ

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

end\_page\_number: intоВЪ

file\_id: Optional[str]оВЪ

start\_page\_number: intоВЪ

type: Literal["page\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]оВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationsWebSearchResultLocation: вА¶оВЪ

cited\_text: strоВЪ

encrypted\_index: strоВЪ

title: Optional[str]оВЪ

type: Literal["web\_search\_result\_location"]оВЪ

url: strоВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оА™

search\_result\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

title: Optional[str]оВЪ

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]оВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

class BetaCompactionBlock: вА¶оВЪ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

content: Optional[str]оВЪ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

encrypted\_content: Optional[str]оВЪ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

type: Literal["compaction"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaFallbackBlock: вА¶оВЪ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn where no preceding model ran and declined has no such
boundary and carries no block вАФ the signal for whether a fallback model
served the response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оА™

from\_: [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)оВЪ

The model whose output ends at this point вАФ the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оА™

model: [Model](api/messages.md)оВЪ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

оА™

оА™

role: Literal["assistant"]оВЪ

Conversational role of the generated message.

This will always be `"assistant"`.

оА™

stop\_details: Optional[BetaRefusalStopDetails]оВЪ

Structured information about a refusal.

оА™

category: Optional[Literal["cyber", "bio", "frontier\_llm", "reasoning\_extraction"]]оВЪ

The policy category that triggered a refusal.

One of the following:

"cyber"оВЪ

"bio"оВЪ

"frontier\_llm"оВЪ

"reasoning\_extraction"оВЪ

оА™

explanation: Optional[str]оВЪ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оА™

fallback\_credit\_token: Optional[str]оВЪ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вАФ which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вАФ if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оА™

fallback\_has\_prefill\_claim: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вАФ
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

recommended\_model: Optional[str]оВЪ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

type: Literal["refusal"]оВЪ

оА™

stop\_reason: Optional[BetaStopReason]оВЪ

The reason that we stopped.

This may be one the following values:

- `"end_turn"`: the model reached a natural stopping point
- `"max_tokens"`: we exceeded the requested `max_tokens` or the model's maximum
- `"stop_sequence"`: one of your provided custom `stop_sequences` was generated
- `"tool_use"`: the model invoked one or more tools
- `"pause_turn"`: we paused a long-running turn. You may provide the response back as-is in a subsequent request to let the model continue.
- `"refusal"`: when streaming classifiers intervene to handle potential policy violations

In non-streaming mode this value is always non-null. In streaming mode, it is null in the `message_start` event and non-null otherwise.

One of the following:

"end\_turn"оВЪ

"max\_tokens"оВЪ

"stop\_sequence"оВЪ

"tool\_use"оВЪ

"pause\_turn"оВЪ

"compaction"оВЪ

"refusal"оВЪ

"model\_context\_window\_exceeded"оВЪ

оА™

stop\_sequence: Optional[str]оВЪ

Which custom stop sequence was generated, if any.

This value will be a non-null string if one of your custom stop sequences was generated.

оА™

type: Literal["message"]оВЪ

Object type.

For Messages, this is always `"message"`.

оА™

usage: [BetaUsage](api/beta.md)оВЪ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

inference\_geo: Optional[str]оВЪ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оА™

iterations: Optional[BetaIterationsUsage]оВЪ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaMessageIterationUsage: вА¶оВЪ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оА™

output\_tokens\_details: Optional[BetaOutputTokensDetails]оВЪ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вАФ for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оА™

thinking\_tokens: intоВЪ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always вЙ§ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

stop\_details: Optional[BetaRefusalStopDetails]оВЪ

Structured information about a refusal.

оА™

category: Optional[Literal["cyber", "bio", "frontier\_llm", "reasoning\_extraction"]]оВЪ

The policy category that triggered a refusal.

One of the following:

"cyber"оВЪ

"bio"оВЪ

"frontier\_llm"оВЪ

"reasoning\_extraction"оВЪ

оА™

explanation: Optional[str]оВЪ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оА™

fallback\_credit\_token: Optional[str]оВЪ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вАФ which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вАФ if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оА™

fallback\_has\_prefill\_claim: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вАФ
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

recommended\_model: Optional[str]оВЪ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

type: Literal["refusal"]оВЪ

оА™

"tool\_use"оВЪ

"pause\_turn"оВЪ

"compaction"оВЪ

"model\_context\_window\_exceeded"оВЪ

usage: [BetaMessageDeltaUsage](api/beta.md)оВЪ

Billing and rate-limit usage.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts, as tokens represent the underlying cost to our systems.

Under the hood, the API transforms requests into a format suitable for the model. The model's output then goes through a parsing stage before becoming an API response. As a result, the token counts in `usage` will not match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response.

For example, `output_tokens` will be non-zero, even for an empty string response from Claude.

Total input tokens in a request is the summation of `input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens`, and `cache_read_input_tokens`.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The cumulative number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The cumulative number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The cumulative number of input tokens which were used.

оА™

iterations: Optional[BetaIterationsUsage]оВЪ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaMessageIterationUsage: вА¶оВЪ

Token usage for a sampling iteration.

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оА™

class BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage: вА¶оВЪ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

output\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of output tokens which were used.

type: Literal["fallback\_message"]оВЪ

Usage for the fallback-model attempt that served the response

output\_tokens: intоВЪ

The cumulative number of output tokens which were used.

оА™

output\_tokens\_details: Optional[BetaOutputTokensDetails]оВЪ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вАФ for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оА™

thinking\_tokens: intоВЪ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always вЙ§ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оА™

server\_tool\_use: Optional[BetaServerToolUsage]оВЪ

The number of server tool requests.

web\_fetch\_requests: intоВЪ

The number of web fetch tool requests.

web\_search\_requests: intоВЪ

The number of web search tool requests.

оА™

class BetaTextBlock: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]оВЪ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

оА™

document\_index: intоВЪ

end\_char\_index: intоВЪ

document\_index: intоВЪ

end\_page\_number: intоВЪ

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]оВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

encrypted\_index: strоВЪ

title: Optional[str]оВЪ

url: strоВЪ

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оА™

search\_result\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]оВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

class BetaCompactionBlock: вА¶оВЪ

A compaction block returned when autocompact is triggered.

When content is None, it indicates the compaction failed to produce a valid
summary (e.g., malformed output from the model). Clients may round-trip
compaction blocks with null content; the server treats them as no-ops.

content: Optional[str]оВЪ

Summary of compacted content, or null if compaction failed

encrypted\_content: Optional[str]оВЪ

Opaque metadata from prior compaction, to be round-tripped verbatim

type: Literal["compaction"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaFallbackBlock: вА¶оВЪ

Marks the point in `content` where one model's output gives way to the next.

One block appears per hop where a preceding model actually ran this turn and
declined. A turn where no preceding model ran and declined has no such
boundary and carries no block вАФ the signal for whether a fallback model
served the response is the presence of a `fallback_message` entry in
`usage.iterations`, not this block.

The block is treated like a server-tool content block for streaming: it
arrives via the standard `content_block_start` / `content_block_stop`
pair and carries no deltas.

оА™

from\_: [BetaFallbackInfo](api/beta.md)оВЪ

The model whose output ends at this point вАФ the model that declined at this hop. When the declining hop is the requested model, its `model` echoes the top-level `model` string the caller sent (alias or canonical); when the declining hop is a fallback model, its `model` is that model's canonical id.

оА™

model: [Model](api/messages.md)оВЪ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

оА™

Literal["claude-fable-5", "claude-mythos-5", "claude-opus-4-8", 12 more]оВЪ

- `claude-mythos-5` - Most capable model for cybersecurity and biology research
- `claude-opus-4-8` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding

class BetaThinkingDelta: вА¶оВЪ

estimated\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

Per-frame increment of a coarse, running estimate of the tokens this thinking block has produced so far. Present whenever the `thinking-token-count-2026-05-13` beta is set; `null` unless `thinking.display` resolves to `"omitted"` and a count is due this frame. Sum the increments across `thinking_delta` frames on this block for a progress indicator. Each increment is a non-negative multiple of a fixed quantum and the cadence is rate-limited, so this is a deliberately lossy display hint, not a billable count; `usage.output_tokens` remains authoritative.

thinking: strоВЪ

type: Literal["thinking\_delta"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaRefusalStopDetails: вА¶оВЪ

Structured information about a refusal.

оА™

category: Optional[Literal["cyber", "bio", "frontier\_llm", "reasoning\_extraction"]]оВЪ

The policy category that triggered a refusal.

One of the following:

"cyber"оВЪ

"bio"оВЪ

"frontier\_llm"оВЪ

"reasoning\_extraction"оВЪ

оА™

explanation: Optional[str]оВЪ

Human-readable explanation of the refusal.

This text is not guaranteed to be stable. `null` when no explanation is available for the category.

оА™

fallback\_credit\_token: Optional[str]оВЪ

Opaque code that refunds the cache-miss cost when retrying this refused
request on the fallback model. Pass it as `fallback_credit_token` on the
retry request. Expires 5 minutes after the refusal.

The retry is sent either with the same request body (`system`, `messages`,
`tools`, and other render-shaping fields), or with the same body plus one
appended `assistant` message whose content is the partial text (with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block) and paired
server-tool blocks from this refusal вАФ which also authorizes that
appended turn as an assistant-prefill continuation on models that otherwise
disallow prefill. A token minted mid-server-tool-loop whose partial content
was continuable may only be redeemed the second way вАФ if a same-body retry
is rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by continuing the
partial response, retry the second way instead. Either way: same workspace,
same platform; a mismatch is a 400. Resending a token for an already-warm
prefix is permitted but yields no additional credit.

`null` when the refused model isn't eligible for a fallback credit.

оА™

fallback\_has\_prefill\_claim: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether the accompanying `fallback_credit_token` may be redeemed with the
appended-assistant retry form. Only set when `fallback_credit_token` is
present.

`true`: retry by resending the same request body plus one appended
`assistant` message whose content is this response's `content` with any
trailing whitespace stripped from the final text block and unpaired
`tool_use` blocks omitted (the same appended-turn shape described on
`fallback_credit_token`), with the token attached. `false`: retry by
resending the original request body unchanged, with the token attached вАФ
the appended-assistant form is not available for this refusal (no
continuable partial content, or the request uses `output_format` or a
`tool_choice` that forces tool use). One exception: when the request used
`output_format` or a forced `tool_choice` and the refusal arrived after
server tools (including MCP connector tools) had already executed, the
token may not be redeemable by either retry form; if the exact-body retry
is then rejected with a 400 saying the token must be redeemed by
continuing the partial response, discard the token and retry without it.

Advisory: if an appended-assistant retry is rejected with a 400 despite
`true`, fall back to resending the original request body with the token.

recommended\_model: Optional[str]оВЪ

The server's suggested retry target for this refusal. Populated when a fallback attempt could not be made (the fallback model's rate limit was exhausted, or it was overloaded); names the fallback model the caller can retry directly. Null otherwise.

type: Literal["refusal"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

оА™

search\_result\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

type: Literal["search\_result\_location"]оВЪ

citations: Optional[List[[BetaTextCitation](api/beta.md)]]оВЪ

Citations supporting the text block.

The type of citation returned will depend on the type of document being cited. Citing a PDF results in `page_location`, plain text results in `char_location`, and content document results in `content_block_location`.

One of the following:

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocation: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

file\_id: Optional[str]оВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

class BetaThinkingConfigAdaptive: вА¶оВЪ

type: Literal["adaptive"]оВЪ

оА™

display: Optional[Literal["summarized", "omitted"]]оВЪ

Controls how thinking content appears in the response. When set to `summarized`, thinking is returned normally. When set to `omitted`, thinking content is redacted but a signature is returned for multi-turn continuity. Defaults to `summarized`.

One of the following:

"summarized"оВЪ

"omitted"оВЪ

оА™

class BetaThinkingConfigDisabled: вА¶оВЪ

type: Literal["disabled"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaThinkingConfigEnabled: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

budget\_tokens: intоВЪ

Determines how many tokens Claude can use for its internal reasoning process. Larger budgets can enable more thorough analysis for complex problems, improving response quality.

Must be вЙ•1024 and less than `max_tokens`.

See [extended thinking](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/extended-thinking) for details.

minimum1024

type: Literal["enabled"]оВЪ

оА™

display: Optional[Literal["summarized", "omitted"]]оВЪ

Controls how thinking content appears in the response. When set to `summarized`, thinking is returned normally. When set to `omitted`, thinking content is redacted but a signature is returned for multi-turn continuity. Defaults to `summarized`.

One of the following:

"summarized"оВЪ

"omitted"оВЪ

оА™

[BetaThinkingConfigParam](api/beta.md)оВЪ

Configuration for enabling Claude's extended thinking.

When enabled, responses include `thinking` content blocks showing Claude's thinking process before the final answer. Requires a minimum budget of 1,024 tokens and counts towards your `max_tokens` limit.

See [extended thinking](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/extended-thinking) for details.

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaThinkingConfigEnabled: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

budget\_tokens: intоВЪ

Determines how many tokens Claude can use for its internal reasoning process. Larger budgets can enable more thorough analysis for complex problems, improving response quality.

Must be вЙ•1024 and less than `max_tokens`.

See [extended thinking](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/extended-thinking) for details.

minimum1024

type: Literal["enabled"]оВЪ

оА™

display: Optional[Literal["summarized", "omitted"]]оВЪ

Controls how thinking content appears in the response. When set to `summarized`, thinking is returned normally. When set to `omitted`, thinking content is redacted but a signature is returned for multi-turn continuity. Defaults to `summarized`.

One of the following:

"summarized"оВЪ

"omitted"оВЪ

оА™

display: Optional[Literal["summarized", "omitted"]]оВЪ

class BetaThinkingDelta: вА¶оВЪ

estimated\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

Per-frame increment of a coarse, running estimate of the tokens this thinking block has produced so far. Present whenever the `thinking-token-count-2026-05-13` beta is set; `null` unless `thinking.display` resolves to `"omitted"` and a count is due this frame. Sum the increments across `thinking_delta` frames on this block for a progress indicator. Each increment is a non-negative multiple of a fixed quantum and the cadence is rate-limited, so this is a deliberately lossy display hint, not a billable count; `usage.output_tokens` remains authoritative.

thinking: strоВЪ

type: Literal["thinking\_delta"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaThinkingTurns: вА¶оВЪ

type: Literal["thinking\_turns"]оВЪ

value: intоВЪ

оА™

class BetaTokenTaskBudget: вА¶оВЪ

User-configurable total token budget across contexts.

total: intоВЪ

Total token budget across all contexts in the session.

type: Literal["tokens"]оВЪ

The budget type. Currently only 'tokens' is supported.

remaining: Optional[int]оВЪ

Remaining tokens in the budget. Use this to track usage across contexts when implementing compaction client-side. Defaults to total if not provided.

оА™

class BetaTool: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

input\_schema: InputSchemaоВЪ

[JSON schema](https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12) for this tool's input.

This defines the shape of the `input` that your tool accepts and that the model will produce.

type: Literal["object"]оВЪ

properties: Optional[Dict[str, object]]оВЪ

required: Optional[List[str]]оВЪ

оА™

name: strоВЪ

Name of the tool.

This is how the tool will be called by the model and in `tool_use` blocks.

maxLength128

minLength1

оА™

allowed\_callers: Optional[List[Literal["direct", "code\_execution\_20250825", "code\_execution\_20260120", "code\_execution\_20260521"]]]оВЪ

"code\_execution\_20250825"оВЪ

"code\_execution\_20260120"оВЪ

"code\_execution\_20260521"оВЪ

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

type: Literal["ephemeral"]оВЪ

оА™

ttl: Optional[Literal["5m", "1h"]]оВЪ

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

"5m"оВЪ

"1h"оВЪ

defer\_loading: Optional[bool]оВЪ

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

оА™

description: Optional[str]оВЪ

Description of what this tool does.

Tool descriptions should be as detailed as possible. The more information that the model has about what the tool is and how to use it, the better it will perform. You can use natural language descriptions to reinforce important aspects of the tool input JSON schema.

eager\_input\_streaming: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Enable eager input streaming for this tool. When true, tool input parameters will be streamed incrementally as they are generated, and types will be inferred on-the-fly rather than buffering the full JSON output. When false, streaming is disabled for this tool even if the fine-grained-tool-streaming beta is active. When null (default), uses the default behavior based on beta headers.

input\_examples: Optional[List[Dict[str, object]]]оВЪ

strict: Optional[bool]оВЪ

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

type: Optional[Literal["custom"]]оВЪ

оА™

[BetaToolChoice](api/beta.md)оВЪ

How the model should use the provided tools. The model can use a specific tool, any available tool, decide by itself, or not use tools at all.

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaToolChoiceAuto: вА¶оВЪ

The model will automatically decide whether to use tools.

type: Literal["auto"]оВЪ

оА™

disable\_parallel\_tool\_use: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether to disable parallel tool use.

Defaults to `false`. If set to `true`, the model will output at most one tool use.

оА™

class BetaToolChoiceAny: вА¶оВЪ

The model will use any available tools.

type: Literal["any"]оВЪ

оА™

disable\_parallel\_tool\_use: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether to disable parallel tool use.

Defaults to `false`. If set to `true`, the model will output exactly one tool use.

оА™

class BetaToolChoiceTool: вА¶оВЪ

The model will use the specified tool with `tool_choice.name`.

name: strоВЪ

The name of the tool to use.

type: Literal["tool"]оВЪ

оА™

disable\_parallel\_tool\_use: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether to disable parallel tool use.

Defaults to `false`. If set to `true`, the model will output exactly one tool use.

оА™

class BetaToolChoiceNone: вА¶оВЪ

The model will not be allowed to use tools.

type: Literal["none"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaToolChoiceAny: вА¶оВЪ

The model will use any available tools.

type: Literal["any"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationWebSearchResultLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оА™

search\_result\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

source: strоВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

title: Optional[str]оВЪ

type: Literal["search\_result\_location"]оВЪ

class BetaCitationWebSearchResultLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оА™

search\_result\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

source: strоВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

title: Optional[str]оВЪ

type: Literal["search\_result\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

[BetaToolUnion](api/beta.md)оВЪ

Code execution tool with REPL state persistence (daemon mode + gVisor checkpoint).

One of the following:

оА™

class BetaTool: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

input\_schema: InputSchemaоВЪ

[JSON schema](https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12) for this tool's input.

This defines the shape of the `input` that your tool accepts and that the model will produce.

type: Literal["object"]оВЪ

properties: Optional[Dict[str, object]]оВЪ

required: Optional[List[str]]оВЪ

оА™

name: strоВЪ

Name of the tool.

This is how the tool will be called by the model and in `tool_use` blocks.

maxLength128

minLength1

оА™

allowed\_callers: Optional[List[Literal["direct", "code\_execution\_20250825", "code\_execution\_20260120", "code\_execution\_20260521"]]]оВЪ

One of the following:

"direct"оВЪ

"code\_execution\_20250825"оВЪ

"code\_execution\_20260120"оВЪ

"code\_execution\_20260521"оВЪ

оА™

cache\_control: Optional[BetaCacheControlEphemeral]оВЪ

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

type: Literal["ephemeral"]оВЪ

оА™

ttl: Optional[Literal["5m", "1h"]]оВЪ

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

"5m"оВЪ

"1h"оВЪ

defer\_loading: Optional[bool]оВЪ

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

оА™

description: Optional[str]оВЪ

Description of what this tool does.

Tool descriptions should be as detailed as possible. The more information that the model has about what the tool is and how to use it, the better it will perform. You can use natural language descriptions to reinforce important aspects of the tool input JSON schema.

eager\_input\_streaming: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Enable eager input streaming for this tool. When true, tool input parameters will be streamed incrementally as they are generated, and types will be inferred on-the-fly rather than buffering the full JSON output. When false, streaming is disabled for this tool even if the fine-grained-tool-streaming beta is active. When null (default), uses the default behavior based on beta headers.

input\_examples: Optional[List[Dict[str, object]]]оВЪ

strict: Optional[bool]оВЪ

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

type: Optional[Literal["custom"]]оВЪ

оА™

"code\_execution\_20260120"оВЪ

"code\_execution\_20260521"оВЪ

allowed\_domains: Optional[List[str]]оВЪ

If provided, only these domains will be included in results. Cannot be used alongside `blocked_domains`.

blocked\_domains: Optional[List[str]]оВЪ

If provided, these domains will never appear in results. Cannot be used alongside `allowed_domains`.

оА™

cache\_control: Optional[BetaCacheControlEphemeral]оВЪ

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

type: Literal["ephemeral"]оВЪ

- `1h`: 1 hour

"5m"оВЪ

"1h"оВЪ

defer\_loading: Optional[bool]оВЪ

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

max\_uses: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

strict: Optional[bool]оВЪ

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

оА™

user\_location: Optional[BetaUserLocation]оВЪ

Parameters for the user's location. Used to provide more relevant search results.

type: Literal["approximate"]оВЪ

city: Optional[str]оВЪ

The city of the user.

country: Optional[str]оВЪ

The two letter [ISO country code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2) of the user.

region: Optional[str]оВЪ

The region of the user.

timezone: Optional[str]оВЪ

The [IANA timezone](https://nodatime.org/TimeZones) of the user.

оА™

"code\_execution\_20260120"оВЪ

"code\_execution\_20260521"оВЪ

List of domains to allow fetching from

blocked\_domains: Optional[List[str]]оВЪ

List of domains to block fetching from

оА™

cache\_control: Optional[BetaCacheControlEphemeral]оВЪ

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

type: Literal["ephemeral"]оВЪ

citations: Optional[BetaCitationsConfigParam]оВЪ

Citations configuration for fetched documents. Citations are disabled by default.

enabled: Optional[bool]оВЪ

defer\_loading: Optional[bool]оВЪ

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

max\_content\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of tokens used by including web page text content in the context. The limit is approximate and does not apply to binary content such as PDFs.

max\_uses: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

strict: Optional[bool]оВЪ

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

blocked\_domains: Optional[List[str]]оВЪ

List of domains to block fetching from

оА™

cache\_control: Optional[BetaCacheControlEphemeral]оВЪ

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

type: Literal["ephemeral"]оВЪ

оА™

ttl: Optional[Literal["5m", "1h"]]оВЪ

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

"5m"оВЪ

"1h"оВЪ

оА™

citations: Optional[BetaCitationsConfigParam]оВЪ

Citations configuration for fetched documents. Citations are disabled by default.

enabled: Optional[bool]оВЪ

defer\_loading: Optional[bool]оВЪ

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

max\_content\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of tokens used by including web page text content in the context. The limit is approximate and does not apply to binary content such as PDFs.

max\_uses: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

strict: Optional[bool]оВЪ

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

use\_cache: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether to use cached content. Set to false to bypass the cache and fetch fresh content. Only set to false when the user explicitly requests fresh content or when fetching rapidly-changing sources.

оА™

"code\_execution\_20260120"оВЪ

cache\_control: Optional[BetaCacheControlEphemeral]оВЪ

Create a cache control breakpoint at this content block.

type: Literal["ephemeral"]оВЪ

- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

"5m"оВЪ

"1h"оВЪ

оА™

caching: Optional[BetaCacheControlEphemeral]оВЪ

Caching for the advisor's own prompt. When set, each advisor call writes a cache entry at the given TTL so subsequent calls in the same conversation read the stable prefix. When omitted, the advisor prompt is not cached.

type: Literal["ephemeral"]оВЪ

оА™

ttl: Optional[Literal["5m", "1h"]]оВЪ

The time-to-live for the cache control breakpoint.

This may be one the following values:

- `5m`: 5 minutes
- `1h`: 1 hour

Defaults to `5m`.

One of the following:

"5m"оВЪ

"1h"оВЪ

defer\_loading: Optional[bool]оВЪ

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

max\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

Bounds the advisor's total output (thinking + text) per call. When the advisor hits this cap, the returned advisor\_result or advisor\_redacted\_result block carries stop\_reason='max\_tokens', and a truncation note is appended to the advice text the worker model sees (inside the encrypted blob in redacted mode). When set, the server also emits a remaining-tokens budget block in the advisor's prompt so the advisor self-shapes toward the cap. When omitted, the advisor model's default output cap applies and no budget block is emitted.

max\_uses: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

strict: Optional[bool]оВЪ

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

оА™

class BetaUsage: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

inference\_geo: Optional[str]оВЪ

The geographic region where inference was performed for this request.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оА™

iterations: Optional[BetaIterationsUsage]оВЪ

Per-iteration token usage breakdown.

Each entry represents one sampling iteration, with its own input/output token counts and cache statistics. This allows you to:

- Determine which iterations exceeded long context thresholds (>=200k tokens)
- Calculate the true context window size from the last iteration
- Understand token accumulation across server-side tool use loops

One of the following:

оА™

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

class BetaFallbackMessageIterationUsage: вА¶оВЪ

Token usage for the fallback-model attempt of a server-side fallback request.

Produced in place of a `message` entry for whichever hop served the
response. A declined hop produces the existing `message` entry. Whether
a fallback model served the response is signalled by the presence of this
entry in `usage.iterations`.

оА™

cache\_creation: Optional[BetaCacheCreation]оВЪ

Breakdown of cached tokens by TTL

ephemeral\_1h\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 1 hour cache entry.

ephemeral\_5m\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the 5 minute cache entry.

cache\_creation\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens used to create the cache entry.

cache\_read\_input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens read from the cache.

input\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of input tokens which were used.

оА™

model: [Model](api/messages.md)оВЪ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

One of the following:

оА™

Literal["claude-fable-5", "claude-mythos-5", "claude-opus-4-8", 12 more]оВЪ

The model that will complete your prompt.

See [models](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/models-overview) for additional details and options.

- `claude-fable-5` - Next generation of intelligence for the hardest knowledge work and coding problems
- `claude-mythos-5` - Most capable model for cybersecurity and biology research
- `claude-opus-4-8` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-7` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-mythos-preview` - Deprecated: Will reach end-of-life on June 30, 2026. Please migrate to claude-mythos-5. Visit <https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/resources/model-deprecations> for more information.
- `claude-opus-4-6` - Frontier intelligence for long-running agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-6` - Best combination of speed and intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` - Fastest model with near-frontier intelligence
- `claude-opus-4-5` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` - Premium model combining maximum intelligence with practical performance
- `claude-sonnet-4-5` - High-performance model for agents and coding
- `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` - High-performance model for agents and coding
- `claude-opus-4-1` - Deprecated: Will reach end-of-life on August 5, 2026. Please migrate to a newer model. Visit <https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/resources/model-deprecations> for more information.
- `claude-opus-4-1-20250805` - Deprecated: Will reach end-of-life on August 5, 2026. Please migrate to a newer model. Visit <https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/resources/model-deprecations> for more information.

strоВЪ

output\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of output tokens which were used.

type: Literal["fallback\_message"]оВЪ

Usage for the fallback-model attempt that served the response

output\_tokens: intоВЪ

The number of output tokens which were used.

оА™

output\_tokens\_details: Optional[BetaOutputTokensDetails]оВЪ

Breakdown of output tokens by category.

`output_tokens` remains the inclusive, authoritative total used for billing.
This object provides a read-only decomposition for observability вАФ for example,
how many of the billed output tokens were spent on internal reasoning that may
have been summarized before being returned to you.

оА™

thinking\_tokens: intоВЪ

Number of output tokens the model generated as internal reasoning, including
the thinking-block delimiter tokens.

Reflects the raw reasoning the model produced, not the (possibly shorter)
summarized thinking text returned in the response body. Computed by
re-tokenizing the raw reasoning text, so it may differ from the model's exact
generation count by a small number of tokens. Always вЙ§ `output_tokens`;
`output_tokens - thinking_tokens` approximates the non-reasoning output.

minimum0

оА™

web\_search\_requests: intоВЪ

The number of web search tool requests.

One of the following:

"standard"оВЪ

"priority"оВЪ

"batch"оВЪ

class BetaCitationContentBlockLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationWebSearchResultLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

cited\_text: strоВЪ

encrypted\_index: strоВЪ

title: Optional[str]оВЪ

type: Literal["web\_search\_result\_location"]оВЪ

url: strоВЪ

оА™

class BetaCitationSearchResultLocationParam: вА¶оВЪ

оА™

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

оА™

search\_result\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the cited search result among all `search_result` content blocks in the request, in the order they appear across messages and tool results.

Counted separately from `document_index`; server-side web search results are not included in this count.

minimum0

source: strоВЪ

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

title: Optional[str]оВЪ

type: Literal["search\_result\_location"]оВЪ

оА™

citations: Optional[BetaCitationsConfigParam]оВЪ

Citations configuration for fetched documents. Citations are disabled by default.

enabled: Optional[bool]оВЪ

defer\_loading: Optional[bool]оВЪ

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

max\_content\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of tokens used by including web page text content in the context. The limit is approximate and does not apply to binary content such as PDFs.

max\_uses: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

strict: Optional[bool]оВЪ

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

citations: Optional[BetaCitationsConfigParam]оВЪ

Citations configuration for fetched documents. Citations are disabled by default.

enabled: Optional[bool]оВЪ

defer\_loading: Optional[bool]оВЪ

If true, tool will not be included in initial system prompt. Only loaded when returned via tool\_reference from tool search.

max\_content\_tokens: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of tokens used by including web page text content in the context. The limit is approximate and does not apply to binary content such as PDFs.

max\_uses: Optional[int]оВЪ

Maximum number of times the tool can be used in the API request.

strict: Optional[bool]оВЪ

When true, guarantees schema validation on tool names and inputs

use\_cache: Optional[bool]оВЪ

Whether to use cached content. Set to false to bypass the cache and fetch fresh content. Only set to false when the user explicitly requests fresh content or when fetching rapidly-changing sources.

cited\_text: strоВЪ

The full text of the cited block range, concatenated.

Always equals the contents of `content[start_block_index:end_block_index]` joined together. The text block is the minimal citable unit; this field is never a substring of a single block. Not counted toward output tokens, and not counted toward input tokens when sent back in subsequent turns.

document\_index: intоВЪ

document\_title: Optional[str]оВЪ

оА™

end\_block\_index: intоВЪ

Exclusive 0-based end index of the cited block range in the source's `content` array.

Always greater than `start_block_index`; a single-block citation has `end_block_index = start_block_index + 1`.

start\_block\_index: intоВЪ

0-based index of the first cited block in the source's `content` array.

type: Literal["content\_block\_location"]оВЪ

оА™
